CloudIocDescription: GithubUserContent known to be used for malicious purposes detected.

PowerShell is a Windows utility that allows access to many Microsoft APIs within a shell environment. In this case, a script attempted to download a file or script to the local system and then execute it. Malware authors may use this to download items, rename them, execute and delete them with a single command.
A Windows shell was invoked with a raw github repository URL as a command-line argument. This could be used to download additional payloads to assist in bypassing application-whitelisting.

CloudIocDescription: Scripts can concatenate multiple quoted strings into one by using the plus (+) operator. The use of repeated string concatenations in scripts has been used by malware to evade detection.

MavInject is a Microsoft Signed Windows utility that can inject a DLL into a running process. This legitimate utility can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code by injecting it into clean applications bypassing application whitelisting. In this case, mavinject was seen injecting a DLL into a running process.

CommandLineArguments: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump ((Get-Process lsass).Id) C:\Windows\Temp\lsass.dmp full
CloudIocDescription: A process was observed attempting to dump process memory using comsvcs.dll located in %WINDIR%\system32 directory. This technique is used by malware to dump LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) memory, LSASS is the security enforcement agent on Windows. It holds login credentials, access tokens and other permissions in memory. Dumping LSASS memory is used by many APT groups and malware to steal locally cached credentials.

CloudIocDescription: GithubUserContent known to be used for malicious purposes detected.

PowerShell is a Windows utility that allows access to many Microsoft APIs within a shell environment. In this case, a script attempted to download a file or script to the local system and then execute it. Malware authors may use this to download items, rename them, execute and delete them with a single command.
A Windows shell was invoked with a raw github repository URL as a command-line argument. This could be used to download additional payloads to assist in bypassing application-whitelisting.

CloudIocDescription: Scripts can concatenate multiple quoted strings into one by using the plus (+) operator. The use of repeated string concatenations in scripts has been used by malware to evade detection.

MavInject is a Microsoft Signed Windows utility that can inject a DLL into a running process. This legitimate utility can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code by injecting it into clean applications bypassing application whitelisting. In this case, mavinject was seen injecting a DLL into a running process.

CommandLineArguments: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump ((Get-Process lsass).Id) C:\Windows\Temp\lsass.dmp full
CloudIocDescription: A process was observed attempting to dump process memory using comsvcs.dll located in %WINDIR%\system32 directory. This technique is used by malware to dump LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) memory, LSASS is the security enforcement agent on Windows. It holds login credentials, access tokens and other permissions in memory. Dumping LSASS memory is used by many APT groups and malware to steal locally cached credentials.

CloudIocDescription: GithubUserContent known to be used for malicious purposes detected.

PowerShell is a Windows utility that allows access to many Microsoft APIs within a shell environment. In this case, a script attempted to download a file or script to the local system and then execute it. Malware authors may use this to download items, rename them, execute and delete them with a single command.
A Windows shell was invoked with a raw github repository URL as a command-line argument. This could be used to download additional payloads to assist in bypassing application-whitelisting.

CloudIocDescription: Scripts can concatenate multiple quoted strings into one by using the plus (+) operator. The use of repeated string concatenations in scripts has been used by malware to evade detection.

MavInject is a Microsoft Signed Windows utility that can inject a DLL into a running process. This legitimate utility can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code by injecting it into clean applications bypassing application whitelisting. In this case, mavinject was seen injecting a DLL into a running process.

CommandLineArguments: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump ((Get-Process lsass).Id) C:\Windows\Temp\lsass.dmp full
CloudIocDescription: A process was observed attempting to dump process memory using comsvcs.dll located in %WINDIR%\system32 directory. This technique is used by malware to dump LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) memory, LSASS is the security enforcement agent on Windows. It holds login credentials, access tokens and other permissions in memory. Dumping LSASS memory is used by many APT groups and malware to steal locally cached credentials.

CloudIocDescription: A Windows shell was invoked with a raw github repository URL as a command-line argument. This could be used to download additional payloads to assist in bypassing application-whitelisting.

PowerShell is a Windows utility that allows access to many Microsoft APIs within a shell environment. In this case, a script attempted to download a file or script to the local system and then execute it. Malware authors may use this to download items, rename them, execute and delete them with a single command.
GithubUserContent known to be used for malicious purposes detected.

CloudIocDescription: Scripts can concatenate multiple quoted strings into one by using the plus (+) operator. The use of repeated string concatenations in scripts has been used by malware to evade detection.

MavInject is a Microsoft Signed Windows utility that can inject a DLL into a running process. This legitimate utility can be abused by attackers to execute malicious code by injecting it into clean applications bypassing application whitelisting. In this case, mavinject was seen injecting a DLL into a running process.

CommandLineArguments: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll, MiniDump ((Get-Process lsass).Id) C:\Windows\Temp\lsass.dmp full
CloudIocDescription: A process was observed attempting to dump process memory using comsvcs.dll located in %WINDIR%\system32 directory. This technique is used by malware to dump LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) memory, LSASS is the security enforcement agent on Windows. It holds login credentials, access tokens and other permissions in memory. Dumping LSASS memory is used by many APT groups and malware to steal locally cached credentials.
